title: cmd, commit: change --pause default to true
body:
Pause the container by default during commit.  It is safer as it avoids conflicts, and potentially security issues, when another process is accessing the container rootfs.

Originally this was not done because it was a breaking change and rootless containers weren't able to use the freezer cgroup controller. Now that we support only cgroup v2, there is no gap anymore with root (exotic configurations can still use --pause=false).

comments.body: I'm a NACK on requiring `--pause=false` for non-cgroup containers - we should handle that natively. Requiring explicit input is just broken.

> I'm a NACK on requiring `--pause=false` for non-cgroup containers - we should handle that natively. Requiring explicit input is just broken.

how to make explicit that it is a security risk that doesn't happen with a sane configuration?  That defeats this change, because in this way we make explicit that the user accepted the risk and `commit` is safe by default, if we make it only in some cases then we are still vulnerable to race conditions while accessing the rootfs

> > I'm a NACK on requiring `--pause=false` for non-cgroup containers - we should handle that natively. Requiring explicit input is just broken.
> 
> how to make explicit that it is a security risk that doesn't happen with a sane configuration? That defeats this change, because in this way we make explicit that the user accepted the risk and `commit` is safe by default, if we make it only in some cases then we are still vulnerable to race conditions while accessing the rootfs

I mean logically using commit is rare, using no cgroups mode is rare, using both at the same time is does even rarer so I think requiring an explicit arg is fine in practise, we should just make sure the error/docs tells the user what do.

And if we decide to merge it like this/next week before 6.0 we can call it a breaking change if you want.
For reference, I am LGTM on the rest of the PR

@Luap99 I feel like supported configurations should not require outside intervention to function, and `--cgroups=none` is still a supported configuration. I'd be fine with throwing warnings by default that "Safety features have been DISABLED due to a lack of cgroups" or similar of course.

> @Luap99 I feel like supported configurations should not require outside intervention to function, and `--cgroups=none` is still a supported configuration. I'd be fine with throwing warnings by default that "Safety features have been DISABLED due to a lack of cgroups" or similar of course.

how can I differentiate between an explicit `--pause=true` (which I think should still fail with nocgroups) and the default being used without breaking `ContainerCommitOptions`?

I am not happy with the special handling for nocgroups, it makes us unsafe in certain cases and I've reverted to the previous version.

I see that Docker does pause the container by default on commit and there is a `--no-pause` option to run without pausing, so I don't think we should be less safe than Docker.

> I am not happy with the special handling for nocgroups, it makes us unsafe in certain cases and I've reverted to the previous version.
> 
> I see that Docker does pause the container by default on commit and there is a `--no-pause` option to run without pausing, so I don't think we should be less safe than Docker.

We _are_ working on a a different kind of change that should, eventually, make the trade-off of features vs. performance (vs. compatibility) ~moot. Certainly, that code will be unproven and risky for a while, but _maybe_ we don’t need to be so drastic as to break existing scripts in the default configuration.

> Certainly, that code will be unproven and risky for a while, but _maybe_ we don’t need to be so drastic as to break existing scripts in the default configuration.

nocgroups is used rarely, so I don't think we will really break many scripts out there

IMO this is about consistency, tar'ing all files while the container process can write to them can lead to "corrupted" exports as you end up with partial states between files. pause on its own would not safe us from reading half written files if we pause the process during writes, but at least files wont change while we read them which greatly reduces risk.
I said this in Matrix, but I think I've been outvoted and am dropping my NACK
Needs a rebase to pick up GH actions
